steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content:
Before the blank: an independent clause ending with the noun “history”
After the blank: a participial phrase beginning with “the former establishing...”

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content:
A comma precedes a participial phrase that elaborates on a noun (this is an appositive relationship). The comma in choice D correctly introduces the participial phrase “the former establishing...” as an elaboration of “events in US civil rights history.” Choices A (adds unnecessary words like “as the”), B (creates a sentence fragment with no finite verb), and C (provides no punctuation) all violate this rule.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
